Dust, mud, lizards, gravel and yoga.
.
Today I started a five week training block leading up to the @ontheedgeevents Kooralbyn MTB Endurance Series. Kicked things off with a two hour XC slog round the 7km training loop I’ve scoped out at the trails up at @northharbourliving.
.
Faired better than expected, but still a heap of work to be done over the coming weeks.
.
Added an extra couple hundred metres onto each lap to avoid the Sunday morning yoga in the heritage park - can’t imagine my sweaty, puffing form whizzing past every twenty minutes would have helped anyone’s Downward Facing Dog 😳
.
#fromwhereiride #outsideisfree #cyclist #cyclistlife #mountainbiking #trainingwithpurpose #wymtm

Scoped out a 7km-ish XC loop at @northharbourliving to train on for my first mountain biking event in March. Rewarded myself with an icey Hazy AF non-alcoholic beer from @brewdogau.
.
#cyclist #cyclistlife #mountainbike #mountainbiking #alcoholfreebeer #alcoholfreedrinks #alcoholfreelifestyle #wymtm #outsideisfree

Fresh new kicks from @ridecncpts paired with socks from @hucktheworldco - guaranteed to make me go faster, right?

Stoked to finally be back out in the trails after my spill over Christmas. Arm is mostly healed and leg is feeling good. Now I can focus on getting ready for my first ever mountain bike event at @kooralbynmtb at the beginning of March (easing in with a four hour endurance event).
.

Taking some baseline measurements for the start of the year. 
.
Whilst I probably won’t be back on the bike for another week or so while my body heals up I don’t want to use that as an excuse to not start pursuing some semblance of a healthier lifestyle.  Whilst I am by no means obese, I have pretty respectable dad-bod happening and could definitely benefit from a bit of weight loss (even if it just means having to carry less up the hill when riding the bike).
.
Haven’t really got much of a plan at the moment, other than riding the bikes more, I’m hoping that by laying it all out there for all to see it will make me a bit more accountable when I get started in earnest.
.
Whilst I doubt I’ll be jumping on the scales every day, I’ll be using my old @withings scale to keep track of my overall body composition throughout the year.  So here it is, the Week One weigh-in:
.
Weight: 90.1kg
Fat: 22.7%
Muscle: 66.3kg
BMI: 27.5
(for reference I am 181cm tall)
.
Will post a monthly update and hopefully start seeing some of those figures changing in the right direction.
.
#day4 #weightloss #healthylifestyle #fitness #health #progress #withingsscale #withingscommunity

After a year like 2020, one of my goals for this year is to pay more attention to my mental health and emotional well-being.  I’ve always had a less-than-ideal relationship with my mental health, so dedicating time to my emotional resilience can only be a good thing, right?
.
My wife found this Mental Fitness & Well-Being Diary from @resilienceagenda I was super keen to pick one up and give it a go for this year.
.
Like physical fitness, mental fitness is all about learning about and deliberately making time for self-care, recovery and developing an understanding of our emotional world (much like we would our physical world).
.
I’m taking some time today (in amongst cooking a kick-ass Sunday roast) to absorb the theory behind Mental Fitness and the daily strategies for building my emotional well-being, ready to go back to work tomorrow morning.
.
Expect to hear more about this bright teal vegan leather-bound book - I’ve got a good feeling about it!.
.
#day3 #mentalfitness #mentalhealth #resilience

Set & Forget
.
New tee from @hucktheworldco pretty much sums up my maintenance/calibration approach to my full-suspension mountain bike. Apart from an annual service and the occasional pump up of the tyres I pretty much don’t do anything to that bike except ride it. And wash it every now and then.
.
My hardtail XC bike is a whole different ball-game, but I have another t-shirt for that...
.
Happy Saturday everyone 🍻
.
#day2 #setandforget #shellberight #mountainbiker #huckyeah

With a load of good sessions at Bunya under my belt I decided to mix things up a little and break out the 29er hardtail. Whilst the steeper head tube angle, shorter travel and lack of suspension in the rear made some of the descents a little more butt-clenching, I definitely appreciated the larger wheels and better power transfer through the clip-ins on the climbs (even if I didn’t have as much traction with the XC tires at times). I even managed the climb out of Bunker Road (officially called ‘I Hate This One’!) without having to get off and push - which is definitely a first! Such fun - reckon I’ll be riding 50/50 on the 29er and full-suspension rig moving forward.
.
#mountainbike #mountainbiking #mountainbiker #cyclist #cyclistlife #wymtm #fromwhereiride #outsideisfree #worklessridemore #strava #stravacycling #singletrack #xcmtb

Great couple of days getting out on the bike - was determined to venture out of my bubble of favourite trails.
.
Yesterday I ventured south to Gap Creek Reserve behind Mt Coot-ha for the first time in almost seven years. Hills were still as brutal as I remember going up, and equally as fun going down.
.
Today we ticket another one of the bucket list and finally hit up Sugarbag Road in Caloundra. That one definitely exceeded expectations and is going to be added to the regular roster.
.
Blistering heat both days, even at 7 in the morning, but the great thing about drinking non-alcoholic beer is that I could enjoy a frosty frothy one (currently my fave @heapsnormal Quiet XPA) back at the trailhead with a few envious onlookers (maybe I’ll pack a few spares in the esky next time!).
.
#fromwhereiride #outsideisfree #lifebehindbars #mountainbiking #mountainbike #mountainbiker #wymtm #trailstoke #cyclist #cyclistlife #nonalcoholicbeer #alcoholfree #alcoholfreelife

Starting the day right!  It's been at least 5-6 years since I last rode Jurassic (2) and Carnivore (6) at Bunya - both black diamond trails.  Partly because of the scary names 😜, but mainly due to lack of fitness.
.
The fitness still isn't there, but today I put on my big boy pants and gave them both a go.  Will need a couple more practice runs before I do either of them at speed, but managed to get through them both without any spills.
.
Also my first time riding up the rock garden out of Creek Track (4) - small victories 💪🏻.
.
#fromwhereiride #outsideisfree #mountainbiking #cyclist #cyclistlife #wymtm #mtb

At the beginning of October I decided to go alcohol-free for at least a month - no mean feat when you are a self-professed beer enthusiast. I did this previously in August, and it felt great, but I was finding that my options at the local bottleshops were sometimes a little limited if you wanted something other than a non-alcoholic lager (hoppy ales is more my jam). I was stoked to discover @craftzeroau and have just received my first delivery of a selection of beers from around the globe.
.

#craftbeer #craftbeerdrinker #alcoholfree #craftzerolife #mindfuldrinking #sober #boozeless #nonalcoholicbeer #ocsober #soberoctober #alcoholfreebeer

Today we promoted my eldest to 20” wheels and a proper ‘big girl bike’ - picked up a @trekbikesau Precaliber 20 to match my X-Caliber from the good folk down at @trekbicyclewoolloongabba (had to pick out a new @ridebontrager helmet and gloves as well to mark the occasion).
.
It’s a touch on the large side for her at the moment but she’ll grow into it in no time - and besides, after about 5 minutes of getting the measure of it she smashed out 3km as if it was nothing. Looking forward to taking this one out on the cycleway with me as her confidence (and legs!) grows.

Today’s findings:
.
1. I really need to practice unclipping from the pedals when I hit obstacles (especially my left foot)
.
2. The @goproanz Chesty mount is awesome, but I need to adjust the angle of the camera.
.
3. I need to pick a new helmet - more specifically one with a visor. I might as well wear a blindfold when climbing up into the early morning sun.
.
Was a little more cautious out in the trails than usual - mainly because it was my first time in a long time clipping in while off-road - but also things were pretty sketchy after the rain earlier this week.  All-in-all though, a morning of good clean fun (and by ‘clean’ I mean muddy af!).
.
#mountainbike #mountainbiking #mtb #xcmtb #cyclist #cycling #outsideisfree #fromwhereiride

Also picked up a Chesty chest mount for the GoPro today to take out on the mountain biking trails. Hoping the weather forecast holds for tomorrow so I can head out on the 29er - and hopefully not capture myself on video falling off!
.

.
.

.

Century Sunday 💯 .
.
Banked my first 100+ km ride of 2019 this morning - actually my first since the Noosa Classic last August.
.
.
Bit of a last minute decision - wanted to see how I’d fare over a longer distance with the last two months of training under my belt. Really happy with my performance - especially over the last quarter. Even though my legs were definitely feeling fatigued I somehow managed to ride faster and stronger than ever 🤷🏻‍♂️
.
.
My only regret - finishing my ride at about 9am, it’s far too early to crack open a cold beer (it is, right?). Had to make do with a coffee instead.
.
.
Looking forward to getting more of these in on a regular basis - next milestone will be the Imperial Century - 100 miles (160km).
.
.
#wymtm #cyclist #cyclistlife #fromwhereiride #outsideisfree #100km #centurysunday #brisbane #sunrise #gofarnotfast

Took the new stand-up paddle board out for a spin. Did not stand up.

Water was looking particularly good this morning when I was out on my ride so came back down with the family for a little beach time. Water was getting a bit choppy for a novice like me to keep their balance so opted to kneel/kayak on the board instead. Still great fun though.

#sup #suplife #standuppaddleboarding #beach #beachday #sundayfunday #getoutside #outsideisfree #getactive

Turns out there’s a network of gnarly dirt tracks in the scrubland near my house - perfect for finally trying out the 29er with the clip-in pedals I bought back in October.

Of course, at the first technical descent I forgot I was clipped-in and superman’d in slo-mo into a nearby bush. Few scrapes and scratches, and a bit of an achy shoulder (we’ll see tomorrow if it feels any worse) but otherwise all good - certainly didn’t stop me from finishing off my ride!

As far as the clip-ins go, I can definitely see why these @shimanomtb Deore XT pedals cost 3x that of the basic egg-beaters I’ve used previously - absolutely top-notch riding experience (when I remember I’m clipped-in 😜). Definitely looking forward to get out on the trails with the clip-ins! (Also, the @fizikofficial M6 MTB shoes... 👌🏻) #mtb #mtblife #iwasspeedingnowimbleeding #fromwhereiride

New kit day (part 2)!
.
.
Been coveting a @blacksheepcycling jersey for some time now but always seem to miss out on their limited releases. Imagine my excitement when one of the jersey’s I’d previously had my eye on was available - in my size - in their 48 hour sample sale! Shut up and take my money! .
.
Love the colours and the details on the special edition Euro Collection jersey, paying tribute to the Tour of Flanders.
.
. In the words of Black Sheep… “Bearing the words Sempre (Forever) on the chest, this special edition Euro Collection jersey is a tribute to Flanders' Finest Eddy Merckx. Dubbed the Lion of Flanders when he won his 'home' race in 1969, he went on to become the greatest cyclist of his generation. Perhaps ever. A man more than worthy of his legendary status, he carries the burden with honour. And will 'forever' be the butcher's son who became a King.”
.
.
Love it!
. . . #blacksheepcycling #monuments #eurocollection #flanders #cyclechic #cyclingkit #cyclingapparel #cyclestyle #cyclegear #cyclinggear #kitporn #cycleclothing #kitdoping

Couldn’t resist getting one last ride in for 2018!
.
I was going to give it another couple weeks of relaxed road riding before hitting the trails, but after getting out on the mountain bike with kids this morning I was really hankering for a bit of dusty action!
.
First time riding both a hard-tail AND a 29er at Bunyaville, and man, that sure was a slice of fried fun! The @trekbikes X-Caliber 9 delivered the goods - great handling and climbed almost as fast as it descended! What a great way to round out the year.
.
.

No more cycling until the New Year 😫

After a recent third stay in hospital in as many months for recurrent pericarditis (this time being the worst yet) I have been officially banned from all exercise for the next couple of months whilst my new course of medication beds in. 
I had plenty of big plans for 2018 in terms of cycling and fitness in general, and they’ve all more or less fallen flat due to poor health.

Anyways, I’m going to take this time to properly rest and recover and plan something for 2019 - I’ve already got a few ideas floating around my head.

Disappointed I’m not out riding the @brissietothebay this morning - my trip to (and subsequent stay in) hospital earlier in the week has revealed I have pericarditis and a pericardial effusion (Google it - I did), and I at the moment I can’t even climb a flight of stairs without getting short of breath.

Whilst I should make a full recovery, I’m a bit worried about how this is going to affect my participation in the three-day @velothonaus in just over a month’s time. Up until last weekend my training had been going well (if not a smidge behind schedule), and the Brissie to the Bay ride was meant to mark the start of my final month of preparation where I intended to ramp up my efforts.

Doctor’s reckon I ought to be feeling right after a couple weeks, and I’ve got an echo of my heart in just under a week to see how things are going, so hopefully I’ll have a better idea of where I stand then.

As for today though - still feeling pretty crap, and longing to get out on two wheels again.

#cyclistlife #illness #injury #pericarditis #dissapointed

I’ve spent a lot of late nights and early mornings over the past month helping my wife @tamsynjs launch her new side-project - Household.

Born out of a frustration with how hard it can be to easily keep track of everything that needs doing in a busy house - from housework and maintenance, to bills and events - Household is at its core a highly focussed to-do list aimed at simplifying household organisation.  Household is also a community of like-minded individuals, seeking to make ‘adulting’ a bit easier.

As well as lending a hand with the development side of things I’ve also been flexing my creative muscles with the branding.

We’re very much taking a ‘release early, release often’ approach so things may be a little rough around the edges as we push on with getting the platform out there and operating in the wild. Watch this space for updates as more and more features come online.

So why not show some love and head on over to @householdapp.co or visit https://householdapp.co and register for your free account!

That’s the 2018 @cycleofgiving 🚲 complete. Despite my lack of consistent training I managed to achieve my goal of doing it in under four hours (even better since it was slightly longer than the 100km I was expecting). Pretty crappy weather conditions didn’t dampen the spirits of the riders, and the organisers and volunteers did a great job of making sure everything went smoothly. I will definitely be adding this to my 2019 ride calendar.

One thing this has shown me, though, is that I’ve got a heap of work to do over the next month if I have any hope of surviving the Ipswich 100 in April. I’m registered for the ‘Imperial’ course, which is 100 MILES (roughly 160km) of hilly Gold Coast Hinterland 😳. #cycling #cyclist #cyclistlife #isurvived #success

Spent a little time this evening putting together something of a presence for my 2018 cycling challenge.  First up - a cheeky bit of branding.  It certainly won’t be winning any awards, but to me giving the challenge a logo has made things a little bit more legit in my mind.

A lot of the details are still up in the air (I’m already considering shifting the timing from August to November, which means it will be after my 40th birthday), and I’ll probably update the logo at some point, but for now I give you the Far North Queensland Dash - Coolangatta to Cape York.

Part of my strategy for raising support for this ride will revolve around an active social media presence, so I’ve set up another Instagram account at @fnqdash just for the ride (and all of preparation leading up to it). I’ve got the beginnings of a @justgiving page ready to start accepting donations for @dementia_australia and I’ll be sorting out a dedicated website at some point as well.  In the meantime, if you could do me a solid and go follow @fnqdash that would be fantastic.

Thanks!

2018 is going to be a big year for me - I turn 40(!), I finally become an Australian citizen, and I will be cycling some 3,000km to raise money and awareness for @dementia_australia.

I wanted to complete a substantial athletic challenge before my 40th birthday in September and have settled on cycling from Coolangatta on the Queensland/New South Wales border all the way up to Cape York, mainland Australia's most northern point.

The challenge isn't just in the ride itself, though - the really tricky part will be transforming myself from a reasonably unfit, mildly overweight middle-aged guy into an endurance athlete, capable of cycling 200-250km every day for over two weeks.

I am aiming to complete the ride some time in August - exact dates and route are still a work in progress. Over the coming weeks I will be putting together a website to track my progress as well as accept donations. I will also start inviting corporate support for various aspects of the ride and my training. In the meantime you can follow my training progress on @strava (link in my bio).

Whilst my profile states that I am a cyclist, in all honesty and can count on two hands the total number of times I’ve been out on two wheels in 2017.  Usual excuses for not getting out on the road/trails - time, work, kids - but let’s face it, they just don’t cut it.

With less than 11 months until I hit 40, I need to work on getting a regular and sustainable exercise regime going - if not for my health then for my sanity!

Last night’s storms across Brisbane left the air nice and fresh this morning, so I figured no time like the present.

The trails were beautifully quiet out at Bunyaville State Forest this morning - which is just as well as it meant that there was nobody around to witness my physical shortcomings.  Man, am I out of shape!  Multiple stops to catch my breath, and at least one dry wretch.  Still, it gives a good benchmark for improvement over the coming months, and it was still hella fun!

#mountainbike #mtb #fitness #unfit #outtashape #gettingold #trek #singletrack

Pictures simply do not do the craftsmanship of each dish justice. Whilst it's hard to pick a favourite, the BBQ Octopus is unforgettable - anyone that's ever tried it will know what I mean when I say the flavour and texture is mind blowing! Kudos to @tamsyno for researching and finding the place, but I fear we may have peaked too early with our dining on this holiday!

16/10 - would dine again!

Bit of nostalgia - broke out one of the team jerseys from 2012 when a group of us cycled the length of mainland Britain (Land’s End to John O’Groats - 1,600km) to raise money for @cr_uk.

Great memories of a time when I was considerably fitter (I could easily smash out 120km on a Sunday afternoon to blow away a hangover), and by the fit of the jersey, considerably slimmer!

Easily one of the toughest, most memorable and most rewarding physical challenges I’ve undertaken - would love to do something similar again.

Thinking about that time in my life, it has increased my resolve to regain that level of fitness - albeit with less drinking (I’m more of a two-pot screamer these days, and don’t handle my hangovers very well!). #cycling #cyclist #cyclistlife #lejog #longdistance #charity #fundraising #cancerresearchuk #maninlycra #nostalgia #fitness #fitfor2017 #lycraselfie

Nearly two years ago, before I moved back into gainful employment, I was working on building a personal brand for freelance and contract work.  Like many one-man-bands, feeling that it would lend a greater air of legitimacy to me, I fell into the trap of making out that I was bigger than I was (I was a ‘boutique’ agency, and I used the royal ‘we’ a lot in the promotional copy). Whilst there wasn’t necessarily anything wrong with this (plenty of ‘boutique’ agencies do it!) it did feel a little disingenuous.

Fast forward to January 2017, and I miss being able to showcase the work that I have been lucky enough to contribute to over the past decade-plus.  So, I figured it was time to work on my personal brand - not in an effort to illicit work (I’m perfectly happy in my current full-time role) - but to share the fruits of my professional life thus far.

This time, I’m not a small ‘multi-discipline agency’, and my posts will hopefully have a lot more personal insight into the work.  I’ll also use it as an opportunity to highlight some of my personal side-projects that I hope to work on this year.

"I love hill climbs!” - said no cyclist ever

I like to have targets with my cycling, and since I’m pretty time-poor at the moment, long distance rides aren’t really a possibility.  So I’ve decided to combat one of the things I least enjoy about riding (hill climbing) and enter the inaugural @cootthaburn next February - essentially an uphill time-trial that climbs Mount Coot-tha overlooking Brisbane.

As many who know me know, Mount Coot-tha is pretty special to me - it’s where I proposed to my wife, amongst other things - hopefully when all is said and done I won’t hate the place haha.

I'll even try to raise money for charity (watch this space)

Dust, mud, lizards, gravel and yoga.
.
Today I started a five week training block leading up to the @ontheedgeevents Kooralbyn MTB Endurance Series. Kicked things off with a two hour XC slog round the 7km training loop I’ve scoped out at the trails up at @northharbourliving.
.
Faired better than expected, but still a heap of work to be done over the coming weeks.
.
Added an extra couple hundred metres onto each lap to avoid the Sunday morning yoga in the heritage park - can’t imagine my sweaty, puffing form whizzing past every twenty minutes would have helped anyone’s Downward Facing Dog 😳
.
#fromwhereiride #outsideisfree #cyclist #cyclistlife #mountainbiking #trainingwithpurpose #wymtm

.

Today we promoted my eldest to 20” wheels and a proper ‘big girl bike’ - picked up a @trekbikesau Precaliber 20 to match my X-Caliber from the good folk down at @trekbicyclewoolloongabba (had to pick out a new @ridebontrager helmet and gloves as well to mark the occasion).
.
It’s a touch on the large side for her at the moment but she’ll grow into it in no time - and besides, after about 5 minutes of getting the measure of it she smashed out 3km as if it was nothing. Looking forward to taking this one out on the cycleway with me as her confidence (and legs!) grows.
.
#prouddad #startthemyoung #outsideisfree #cyclist #cyclistdad #cyclingdad #dadlife

Today’s findings:
.
1. I really need to practice unclipping from the pedals when I hit obstacles (especially my left foot)
.
2. The @goproanz Chesty mount is awesome, but I need to adjust the angle of the camera.
.
3. I need to pick a new helmet - more specifically one with a visor. I might as well wear a blindfold when climbing up into the early morning sun.
.
Was a little more cautious out in the trails than usual - mainly because it was my first time in a long time clipping in while off-road - but also things were pretty sketchy after the rain earlier this week.  All-in-all though, a morning of good clean fun (and by ‘clean’ I mean muddy af!).
.
#mountainbike #mountainbiking #mtb #xcmtb #cyclist #cycling #outsideisfree #fromwhereiride
